name: Excessive System Tools Usage from a Single Host
notes: This rule triggers when multiple system tools are executed on the same host.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create, (5000862) Success Audit: A new process has been created
when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of Systools - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when at least 7 events are seen with the same Source IP and different Process Name (custom) in 1 hour(s)
thresholds: when at least 7 events are seen with the same Source IP and different Process Name (custom) in 1 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Excessive System Tools Usage from a Single Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001828) Process Create, (5000862) Success Audit: A new process has been created
AND when any of Process Name (custom) are contained in any of Systools - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND when at least 7 events are seen with the same Source IP and different Process Name (custom) in 1 hour(s)
RED_list: Event name: Detected Excessive Usage of System Tools from a Single Machine
Event description: Detected Excessive Usage of System Tools from a Single Machine
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
Add the "sourceIP" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Compromised Hosts"
